data.description:
## Observability gap

`rebar audit show <id> --output json` surfaces the **completion** attestation/sidecar, but its
`plan_reviews[]` entries do **not** expose whether each plan-review was cryptographically
**signed**. The per-entry keys are: `coaching, coverage, findings, impact_model_version,
material_fingerprint, metrics, model, regver, runner, schema, ticket_id, ticket_type, verdict,
verified_at_sha` — there is no `signed` / `signature` / `signer` / `key_id` field.

Consequence: a PASS that was signed is **indistinguishable in the audit** from a PASS produced
with `--no-sign` (or one whose signing step failed to persist). The plan-review **claim gate**
depends on a valid signed attestation existing, yet the audit trail — the natural place to
confirm gate-readiness — cannot answer "is this plan-review signed, by whom, and at what SHA?".

To confirm signature persistence today you must bypass the audit and read the raw
`*-SIGNATURE.json` events on the `tickets` branch (`git show tickets:<id>/<...>-SIGNATURE.json`)
and check the latest SIGNATURE is newer than the latest EDIT. That is not a supported/ergonomic
verification path.

## Suggested fix

Add plan-review signature metadata to each `plan_reviews[]` entry in the audit projection —
e.g. `signed: bool`, `signer` (author id / email), `key_id`, and the attestation
`material_fingerprint` + `verified_at_sha` it covers — mirroring how the completion attestation
is already surfaced. Optionally a `signature_current: bool` (no EDIT newer than the SIGNATURE).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `rebar audit show <id> --output json` `plan_reviews[]` entries include a signature block
  (signed flag + signer + key_id + covered fingerprint/sha) when a `SIGNATURE` event exists.
- [ ] A signed PASS and a `--no-sign` PASS are distinguishable in the audit output.
- [ ] Text output (`--output text`) notes signed/unsigned per plan-review.
data.title: audit show does not surface plan-review signature status (signed PASS indistinguishable from --no-sign)
